 31→ *      3. Doubled after an import path, e.g. "java.**" or "kotlin.io.**", meaning "with subpackages"
 32→ *  * "|" - blank line symbol. Only supported single blank lines between imports. Multiple blank lines will be ignored. Blank lines are not allowed outside of import list.
 33→ *  * "^" - alias symbol, can be used as follows:
 34→ *      1. In front of an import path, meaning matching all alias imports from this path, e.g. "^android.*"
 35→ *      2. Alone, meaning matching any alias import - "^" (<all other alias imports> in IDEA)
 36→ *  * import paths - these can be full paths, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard paths meaning "with subpackages", e.g. "kotlin.**"
 37→ *
 38→ * In case the custom property is not provided, the rule defaults to alphabetical order in case of "android" flag supplied, or to idea otherwise.
 39→ */
 40→public class ImportOrderingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "import-ordering",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY),
 44→    ) {
 45→    private lateinit var importsLayout: List<PatternEntry>
 46→    private lateinit var importSorter: ImportSorter
 47→
 48→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 49→        importsLayout = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY]
 50→        importSorter = ImportSorter(importsLayout)
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 54→        node: ASTNode,
 55→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 56→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 57→    ) {
 58→        if (node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_LIST) {
 59→            val children = node.getChildren(null)
 60→            if (children.isNotEmpty()) {
 61→                // Get unique imports and blank lines
 62→                val (autoCorrectDuplicateImports: Boolean, imports: List<ASTNode>) =
 63→                    getUniqueImportsAndBlankLines(children, emit)
 64→
 65→                val hasComments = children.any { it.elementType == ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT || it.elementType == ElementType.EOL_COMMENT }
 66→                val sortedImports = imports
 67→                    .asSequence()
 68→                    .mapNotNull { it.psi as? KtImportDirective } // sorter expects KtImportDirective, whitespaces are inserted afterwards
 69→                    .sortedWith(importSorter)
 70→                    .map { it.node } // transform back to ASTNode in order to operate over its method (addChild)
 71→
 72→                // insert blank lines wherever needed
 73→                // traverse the list using fold to have previous and current element and decide if the blank line is needed in between
 74→                // based on the ImportSorter imported patterns and indexes from the comparator
 75→                val sortedImportsWithSpaces = mutableListOf<ASTNode>()
 76→                sortedImports.fold(null as ASTNode?) { prev, current ->
 77→                    val index1 = if (prev == null) -1 else importSorter.findImportIndex((prev.psi as KtImportDirective).importPath!!)
 78→                    val index2 = importSorter.findImportIndex((current.psi as KtImportDirective).importPath!!)
 79→
 80→                    var hasBlankLines = false
 81→                    for (i in (index1 + 1) until index2) {
 82→                        if (importSorter.patterns[i] == PatternEntry.BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) {
 83→                            hasBlankLines = true
 84→                            break
 85→                        }
 86→                    }
 87→                    if (hasBlankLines && prev != null) {
 88→                        sortedImportsWithSpaces += PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n\n")
 89→                    }
 90→                    sortedImportsWithSpaces += current
 91→
 92→                    return@fold current
 93→                }
 94→
 95→                if (hasComments) {
 96→                    emit(
 97→                        node.startOffset,
 98→                        ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE) +
 99→                            " -- no autocorrection due to comments in the import list",
100→                        false,
101→                    )
102→                } else {
103→                    val autoCorrectWhitespace = hasTooMuchWhitespace(children) && !isCustomLayout()
104→                    val autoCorrectSortOrder = !importsAreEqual(imports, sortedImportsWithSpaces)
105→                    if (autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace) {
106→                        emit(
107→                            node.startOffset,
108→                            ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE),
109→                            true,
110→                        )
111→                    }
112→                    if (autoCorrect && (autoCorrectDuplicateImports || autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace)) {
113→                        node.removeRange(node.firstChildNode, node.lastChildNode.treeNext)
114→                        sortedImportsWithSpaces.reduce { current, next ->
115→                            node.addChild(current, null)
116→                            if (current !is PsiWhiteSpace && next !is PsiWhiteSpace) {
117→                                node.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n"), null)
118→                            }
119→                            return@reduce next
120→                        }
121→                        node.addChild(sortedImportsWithSpaces.last(), null)
122→                    }
123→                }
124→            }
125→        }
126→    }
127→
128→    private fun getUniqueImportsAndBlankLines(
129→        children: Array<ASTNode>,
130→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
131→    ): Pair<Boolean, List<ASTNode>> {
132→        var autoCorrectDuplicateImports = false
133→        val imports = mutableListOf<ASTNode>()
134→        val importTextSet = mutableSetOf<String>()
135→
136→        children.forEach { current ->
137→            val isPsiWhiteSpace = current.psi is PsiWhiteSpace
138→
139→            if (current.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE ||
140→                isPsiWhiteSpace && current.textLength > 1 // also collect empty lines, that are represented as "\n\n"
141→            ) {
142→                if (isPsiWhiteSpace || importTextSet.add(current.text)) {
143→                    imports += current
144→                } else {
145→                    emit(
146→                        current.startOffset,
147→                        "Duplicate '${current.text}' found",
148→                        true,
149→                    )
150→                    autoCorrectDuplicateImports = true
151→                }
152→            }
153→        }
154→
155→        return autoCorrectDuplicateImports to imports
156→    }
157→
158→    private fun importsAreEqual(
159→        actual: List<ASTNode>,
160→        expected: List<ASTNode>,
161→    ): Boolean {
162→        if (actual.size != expected.size) return false
163→
164→        val combined = actual.zip(expected)
165→        return combined.all { (first, second) ->
166→            if (first is PsiWhiteSpace && second is PsiWhiteSpace) {
167→                return@all (first as PsiWhiteSpace).text == (second as PsiWhiteSpace).text
168→            }
169→            return@all first == second
170→        }
171→    }
172→
173→    private fun isCustomLayout() = importsLayout != IDEA_PATTERN && importsLayout != ASCII_PATTERN
174→
175→    private fun hasTooMuchWhitespace(nodes: Array<ASTNode>): Boolean {
176→        return nodes.any { it is PsiWhiteSpace && (it as PsiWhiteSpace).text != "\n" }
177→    }
178→
179→    public companion object {
180→        /**
181→         * Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a).
182→         * No blank lines between major groups (android, com, junit, net, org, java, javax).
183→         * Single group regardless of import type.
184→         *
185→         * https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#import_statements
186→         */
187→        private val ASCII_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*")
188→
189→        /**
190→         * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a),
191→         * except such groups as "java", "javax" and "kotlin" that are placed in the end. Within the groups the alphabetical order is preserved.
192→         * Alias imports are placed in a separate group in the end of the list with alphabetical order inside.
193→         * No blank lines between groups.
194→         *
195→         * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L87-L91
196→         */
197→        private val IDEA_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^")
198→
199→        private const val IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between " +
200→            "with \"java\", \"javax\", \"kotlin\" and aliases in the end"
201→        private const val ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between"
202→        private const val CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered according to the pattern specified in .editorconfig"
203→
204→        private val ERROR_MESSAGES = mapOf(
205→            IDEA_PATTERN to IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE,
206→            ASCII_PATTERN to ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE,
207→        )
208→
209→        private val EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
210→            { _, value ->
211→                when {
212→                    value.isNullOrBlank() -> PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
213→                        value,
214→                        "Import layout must contain at least one entry of a wildcard symbol (*)",
215→                    )
216→                    value == "idea" -> {
217→                        LOGGER.warn {
218→                            "`idea` is deprecated! Please use `*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE " +
219→                                "plugin recognizes the value"
220→                        }
221→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
222→                            value,
223→                            IDEA_PATTERN,
224→                        )
225→                    }
226→                    value == "ascii" -> {
227→                        LOGGER.warn {
228→                            "`ascii` is deprecated! Please use `*` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE plugin recognizes the value"
229→                        }
230→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
231→                            value,
232→                            ASCII_PATTERN,
233→                        )
234→                    }
235→                    else -> try {
236→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
237→                            value,
238→                            parseImportsLayout(value),
239→                        )
240→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
241→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
242→                            value,
243→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
244→                        )
245→                    }
246→                }
247→            }
248→
249→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
250→            EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>>(
251→                type = PropertyType(
252→                    "ij_kotlin_imports_layout",
253→                    "Defines imports order layout for Kotlin files",
254→                    EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER,
255→                ),
256→                defaultValue = IDEA_PATTERN,
257→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = ASCII_PATTERN,
258→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
259→            )
260→    }
261→}
262→
263→public val IMPORT_ORDERING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ImportOrderingRule().ruleId
264→

import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.ImportOrderingRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.ImportOrderingRule.Companion.IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

/**
 * Tests for the bug fix related to:
 * https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1845
 *
 * `ij_kotlin_imports_layout` with `|` generates multiple line breaks when
 * imports don't match leading patterns in the layout.
 */
class ImportOrderingRuleBlankLinesBugTest {
    private val importOrderingRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { ImportOrderingRule() }

    @Test
    fun `Given imports not matching the first pattern group, no extra blank lines should be added before the imports`() {
        // Pattern: *, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**
        // When there are no imports matching "*", "javax.**", or the first blank line,
        // we should NOT have blank lines before the java imports.
        val code =
            """
            package test

            import java.nio.file.Files
            import java.nio.file.Paths
            import java.util.Objects
            """.trimIndent()
        importOrderingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY to "*, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**")
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given imports with extra blank lines before imports, they should be removed`() {
        // Pattern: *, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**
        // When there are extra blank lines before the imports, they should be removed.
        val code =
            """
            package test



            import java.nio.file.Files
            import java.nio.file.Paths
            import java.util.Objects
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            package test

            import java.nio.file.Files
            import java.nio.file.Paths
            import java.util.Objects
            """.trimIndent()
        importOrderingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY to "*, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given imports matching multiple groups with blank lines between them`() {
        // Pattern: *, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**
        // When we have imports from different groups, blank lines should be between them.
        val code =
            """
            package test

            import foo.Bar

            import java.nio.file.Files
            import java.util.Objects

            import kotlin.collections.List
            """.trimIndent()
        importOrderingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY to "*, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**")
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given imports that need to be reordered with blank lines properly inserted`() {
        // Pattern: *, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**
        val code =
            """
            package test

            import java.nio.file.Files
            import foo.Bar
            import kotlin.collections.List
            import java.util.Objects
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            package test

            import foo.Bar

            import java.nio.file.Files
            import java.util.Objects

            import kotlin.collections.List
            """.trimIndent()
        importOrderingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY to "*, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given only kotlin imports with multi-separator pattern, no extra blank lines should be added`() {
        // Pattern: *, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**
        // Only kotlin imports - should not have blank lines before them.
        val code =
            """
            package test

            import kotlin.collections.List
            import kotlin.io.path.Path
            """.trimIndent()
        importOrderingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY to "*, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**")
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given only kotlinx imports with multi-separator pattern, no extra blank lines should be added`() {
        // Pattern: *, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**
        // Only kotlinx imports - should not have blank lines before them.
        val code =
            """
            package test

            import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
            import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
            """.trimIndent()
        importOrderingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY to "*, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**")
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given pattern with many separators and imports matching the last group only`() {
        // Pattern with many | separators
        val code =
            """
            package test

            import kotlin.collections.List
            """.trimIndent()
        importOrderingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY to "*, |, javax.**, |, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, |, kotlin.**")
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given mixed imports from all groups with proper blank lines`() {
        val code =
            """
            package test

            import foo.Bar
            import org.example.Test

            import javax.annotation.Nullable

            import java.util.List

            import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers

            import kotlin.collections.listOf
            """.trimIndent()
        importOrderingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY to "*, |, javax.**, |, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, |, kotlin.**")
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }
}
